title: TAS topology
description: How the converter is built. A tree of stages (one circuit each) plus the inter-stage connections that wire them together. The root stage takes the converter input; leaves produce regulated outputs. Branching happens at stages with multiple outputPorts (typically isolation in multi-output converters).

interStageCircuit.description: Connections that cross stage boundaries: the wires named by stage ports, magnetic couplings spanning stages (rare), and the converter's externalPorts (Vin+, Vout-, GND, EN, FB).

phaseCount.description: Number of interleaved physical phases sharing one logical stage and one controller. >1 only meaningful for switching stages (pfc, switchingCell, inverter).

allOf.$comment: Power-chain stages need ports; control stages need senses/drives instead.
Only isolation may have more than one output port (multi-output secondaries).

stageRole.description: Functional role. Cascade type-signatures: lineFilter acLine→acLine; rectifier acLine→pulsatingDc; pfc pulsatingDc→dcBus; bulkStorage dcBus→dcBus; switchingCell dcBus→dcBus (non-isolated DC-DC); inverter dcBus→hfAc (owns the resonant tank for LLC/SRC, per Maksimović); isolation hfAc→hfAc[] (branch point for multi-output); outputRectifier hfAc→pulsatingDc; outputFilter pulsatingDc→dcOutput; control has no power-chain position.

wire.description: Name of the boundary wire. Must appear both in this stage's circuit.connections (so internal endpoints can reference it) and in topology.interStageCircuit (so the upstream/downstream stage's port can reference the same name).

name.description: Port label, used to disambiguate when a stage has multiple outputPorts (e.g. 'sec_5V', 'sec_12V' on an isolation stage).

portType.description: acLine = mains-frequency AC. pulsatingDc = rectified, unfiltered. dcBus = filtered DC (regulated or not). hfAc = switching-frequency AC (transformer or resonant tank). dcOutput = final regulated DC output.

component.description: Name of the component being sensed (e.g. a sense resistor, a thermistor).

wire.description: Name of the wire whose voltage is sensed (e.g. 'Vout' for output feedback).

component.description: Name of the component being driven (typically a switch or driver IC).
